channel.title: Sustainability on Lucky Taorem | Tech & AI Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Sustainability on Lucky Taorem | Tech & AI Blog

item.title: Google Buys Solar Farm Output
item.description:
Introduction to Google’s Sustainability Efforts  

Google has recently struck a deal to purchase the entire electrical output from the Steel River Energy Center solar project in Arkansas. This move is aimed at offsetting the company’s fossil fuel emissions, which have been increasing due to the growing demand for its data centers. The Steel River Energy Center solar project is expected to come online in 2029 and will use US-manufactured steel and solar panels.

item.title: New York Bans Data Center Construction
item.description:
Introduction to Data Center Construction Ban  

New York has become the first state in the US to enact a ban on data center construction, aiming to promote environmental sustainability. This move is expected to have significant implications for the tech industry, particularly for companies reliant on large data centers. Governor Kathy Hochul signed an executive order pausing environmental permits for large data centers consuming more than 50 megawatts for up to a year. As reported, “New York will lead the way in creating the strongest standards in the nation for data center development, ensuring that when companies succeed because of New York, New Yorkers succeed too.”

item.title: Tech News Today
item.description:
Introduction to The Download  

The world of technology is constantly evolving, with new innovations and discoveries being made every day. In this article, we will delve into some of the most exciting and groundbreaking developments in the tech industry, including the construction of the world’s longest and deepest subsea road tunnel, the impact of the AI boom on the electric grid, and the latest news on data centers and flexible power consumption.

item.title: Used iPhones Now Better
item.description:
Introduction to the World of Used iPhones  

The concept of buying used iPhones has been around for a while, but it has recently gained more attention due to various factors. The increasing awareness of environmental sustainability, coupled with the financial benefits of purchasing pre-owned devices, has made buying used iPhones a more appealing option. In this article, we will delve into the details of why buying used iPhones has never made more sense, exploring the recent developments that have contributed to this trend.
